title: U.S. Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Most Powerful A.I. Models
summary: The move allows Anthropic to bring its most powerful technologies back online, de-escalating a feud with the Trump administration.

title: Bending Spoons, Owner of AOL and Other Old Internet Brands, Is Going Public
summary: Bending Spoons, an Italian company that buys aging internet companies, is going public this week at a potential value of $19 billion.

title: Neon Buys ‘Artificial,’ a Film About OpenAI, After Amazon Dropped It
summary: Neon purchased “Artificial,” which focuses on OpenAI’s chief, Sam Altman, after Amazon walked away from it following an investment in the start-up.

title: In San Francisco’s A.I. Era, Even $180,000 Tech Salaries Are No Longer Enough
summary: As OpenAI and Anthropic prepare to go public, tech workers making six figures are grousing that they cannot compete with the new A.I. elite. Some doubt they can afford to stay.

title: The Yoto Music Box Is a Ray of Hope Amid the ‘Techlash’
summary: Amid widespread “techlash” over addictive screens and apps, the Yoto, an audio player for children, shows there’s still a way to make money while doing something nice.

title: BMW Will Build a New Electric S.U.V. in South Carolina
summary: The German company is doubling down on electric vehicles even as other automakers pull back after acknowledging billions of dollars in losses.

title: How A.I. Is Changing the Way Politicians Run for Office
summary: A.I.-generated images are the public face of this election overhaul. Behind the scenes, campaigns are using the technology to analyze voter data, craft campaign materials and write custom messages.

title: Many Child Safety Features on Social Apps Don’t Work, Report Finds
summary: Researchers found that teens can discover harmful content, connect with unknown adults and easily bypass time limits.

title: U.S. Loosens Restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos A.I. Model
summary: The move de-escalates a clash between the Trump administration and the company over its cutting-edge artificial intelligence systems.

title: Om Malik, Whose Blog Shaped How Silicon Valley Saw Itself, Dies at 59
summary: Gigaom, which he started in 2001, established him as a leading voice in the tech world and signaled a shift in how the media covered the industry.

title: Mark Zuckerberg Urges Meta to Explore Working With Polymarket and Kalshi
summary: Mr. Zuckerberg’s plans for Arena, a prediction markets app that Meta is building, also include appealing to 18- to 34-year-old users.

title: In Europe, Tesla Sales Are Rising Despite Views on Elon Musk
summary: Price cuts and low-interest-rate loans are luring buyers, including people offended by the company’s chief executive.

title: ‘The Daily’ and ‘The Opinions’: How A.I. Is Changing Loneliness and Taste
summary: The story of a woman who let a robot into her home. Plus, a discussion about why Silicon Valley has taken such an interest in taste.

title: Intel’s Chip Business Shows Signs of Life After Years of Struggle
summary: It is the centerpiece of President Trump’s drive to make more chips in the United States, but the company still has a long way to go before it can be called a complete turnaround.

title: How a Niche Technology Became a Choke Point for A.I.
summary: Advanced chip packaging, which boosts computing power for artificial intelligence, has made the United States more reliant on Taiwan than ever.

title: Trump Plans to Name Adam Candeub as Antitrust Chief in Justice Dept.
summary: President Trump plans to nominate a tech critic and telecommunications lawyer, Adam Candeub, to lead the division.

title: The New York Times Amends Lawsuit Against OpenAI and Microsoft
summary: In a new court filing, The Times accused Microsoft of encouraging OpenAI to train its A.I. systems using copyrighted articles.

title: OpenAI Leans Toward Holding Up I.P.O. Until Next Year
summary: The A.I. company’s advisers are pushing its chief executive, Sam Altman, to move slowly after SpaceX’s stock has been volatile and as the start-up grapples with financial challenges.

title: Chinese A.I. Models Gain Ground on Anthropic and OpenAI
summary: Silicon Valley engineers recently flocked to new technology from a Chinese company, Z.ai, that is almost as good as its American competitors but much cheaper.

title: Apple Raises Prices on Macs and iPads Amid the A.I. Boom
summary: The tech giant cited the soaring costs of memory and storage chips as it increased prices more than $200 on some devices.

title: Larry Sanger, a Wikipedia Founder, Is Barred From Editing on the Site
summary: A vote to bar Larry Sanger indefinitely from the volunteer community of Wikipedia came days after he submitted a proposal on “intellectual diversity.”

title: IBM Unveils New Method to Make Smaller Computer Chip Parts
summary: Industry leaders had worried that innovations in chip miniaturization were no longer possible.

title: OpenAI and Broadcom Unveil Custom A.I. Chip Design
summary: The maker of ChatGPT plans to use enough chips to consume 10 gigawatts of electricity, an amount that could power millions of households.
